note: Preregistration for the Binance source-quality gate (issue #64): bounded Stage-2 integrity certification of the already-local Crypto Lake 2026-04-01 smoke, plus an independent-source (CryptoHFTData) comparison harness. Committed BEFORE any decision-bearing real-data reconstruction, tick measurement, or comparison ran on this branch. The module constant experiments.binance_source_gate.PREREGISTERED is pinned equal to the thresholds/decision_logic/fixture-identity subtrees by tests/test_binance_source_gate.py. Thresholds cannot change after any decision-bearing real-data result has been inspected; amendments are append-only, dated, added before their results are seen, and may only tighten. April 2026 is the G0-XV holdout: only the permitted_april_metrics below may be computed or published for April data; every forbidden_april_metrics item is a hard guard enforced by the report allowlist and by test. This experiment does not authorize the six-month acquisition (#35), any bulk download, or any modeling-outcome inspection.

access.auth: anonymous free tier documented at 60 requests/minute per IP; optional free API key

probe.rationale: mid-day hour chosen a priori (no day-boundary seam); futures first because perp is signal-primary

expansion_day_objects.binance_futures: hours 00..23 of 2026-04-01 (23 further objects after the probe)

permitted_april_metrics: row counts (per unit / per hour / per event_type / dropped / deduplicated / skipped)
sha256 of files, frame_replay_hash of reconstructed frames, report_hash of reports
schema conformance: column names, dtypes, schema fingerprints, KV metadata
engine-time population fractions, chosen engine-time column, engine-time fallback flag, dropped-row counts
timestamp-unit normalization outcome (detected timescale) and monotone-watermark regression counts
sequence aggregates (Lake): non-monotone (ts,seq) pair counts, duplicate-seq fraction (descriptive; seq is not a row-gap detector per docs 5a-Recon)
update-ID continuity (CryptoHFTData): contiguity violation counts, gap counts, overlap counts, duplicate-event counts, reset counts, pre-snapshot skip counts
snapshot validity: candidate counts, classify_snapshot reason-code histograms, seed accepted/reseed counts and timestamps, seed_source_crossed_frac
fixed book-quality metrics on the preregistered grid: crossed_samples/crossed_rate, missing_book_samples/fraction, thin_depth_samples/fraction, crossed_duration_s, n_invalid_runs
stale/frozen-book metrics per the integrity_definitions: frozen run counts, max frozen run seconds, frozen_fraction
delta-stream silence metrics: max inter-event gap seconds, counts of gaps over 10s/60s/300s, total silent seconds
tick conformance: off-tick counts at the expected scale, measured decimal scale per instrument/feed, price-decimals histogram bounds
determinism evidence: cross-run equality booleans, hash equality
python/native conformance evidence: frame and meta equality booleans

cross-source deviation aggregates in tick units (and their fixed USD equivalents): |delta best_bid|, |delta best_ask|, |delta mid| quantiles (p50/p95/p99/max), touch agreement fractions, joint-valid fraction, per-side top-K price-set overlap counts (descriptive)
measured requests, bytes, runtimes, exit codes, failure reasons
forbidden_april_metrics: features, feature matrices, or any bars/features/labels producer output
labels and label agreement (including the issue-54 label_agreement_min family)
costs, cost curves, forecasts, PnL, positions, trade simulations, model outputs or scores
trade-feed distribution metrics: price/size/notional/interarrival/lag/side summaries (ingest/validate_trade_feeds.py-class reports; outcome-bearing per docs/experiment-plan.md Phase 0S)

price or mid or spread LEVEL statistics in currency (means/quantiles/min/max of price levels), returns, realized volatility, price-path series, price-path plots, or manually selected price-path excerpts
publishing or printing raw market rows (prices/sizes of real April rows) in any tracked report, issue comment, PR body, or log
any per-sample time series of April book state in tracked evidence (aggregates only)

lake.book: timestamp, receipt_timestamp, sequence_number, bid_{0..19}_price/size, ask_{0..19}_price/size (20-level snapshot seed)
lake.note: engine-time aliases timestamp->origin_time, receipt_timestamp->received_time canonicalized by ingest.lake_binance.canonicalize_time_columns; per-unit fingerprints pinned in fixture.lake.units

cryptohftdata_common_orderbook_event.received_time: INT64, non-null, unix ns at vendor capture
cryptohftdata_common_orderbook_event.event_time: INT64, non-null, exchange event time (timescale exchange-dependent; normalized per replay_contract.timestamp_units)
cryptohftdata_common_orderbook_event.quantity: STRING decimal, non-null; '0' removes the level
cryptohftdata_common_orderbook_event.order_count: INT64, nullable (optional; ignored by the adapter, presence recorded)
cryptohftdata_common_orderbook_event.row_shape: one row per (event, side, price level); snapshot events span many rows sharing last_update_id

replay_contract.sampling: as-of apply-before-read: every event with engine-time ts <= sample_ts is applied before the sample is emitted (recon.reseed._replay_seeded convention)

lake.engine_time: joint resolution across delta and book-seed frames via ingest.lake_binance.resolve_engine_time (origin_time if 100% populated in every frame, else received_time if 100%, else drop-and-record); chosen column and dropped counts recorded
lake.ordering: np.lexsort((sequence_number, engine_time)) - primary engine_time, secondary sequence_number, stable
lake.snapshot_merge: recon.reseed._merge_time_ordered: at equal ts the delta applies first, the snapshot second (authoritative overwrite)
lake.engine: --engine auto (Python oracle expected: no Binance tick scale is registered in recon/native.py::_TICK_SCALE)
lake.production_path: scripts/run_binance_recon.py, all nine units, defaults k=10 --grid-s 1.0

cryptohftdata.timestamp_units: event_time/received_time/transaction_time normalized to ns by the unique k in {1, 1e3, 1e6, 1e9} multiplier that lands the column median inside [2020-01-01, 2030-01-01) UTC in ns; fail closed if none fits
cryptohftdata.event_order: events ordered by final_update_id ascending (snapshot events keyed by last_update_id); rows grouped into events by (event_type, first_update_id, final_update_id, last_update_id)
cryptohftdata.same_time_tie_break: (final_update_id, side, price-ticks) within the stream; duplicate (side, price) rows inside one event are malformed -> fail closed
cryptohftdata.monotone_watermark: sampling time axis = running max of normalized event_time in update-ID order; raw regression count recorded; regressions > 0.1% of events -> fail closed (ordering anomaly)
cryptohftdata.futures_continuity: after snapshot(last_update_id=L): skip updates with final_update_id < L; first applied update must satisfy (first_update_id <= L <= final_update_id) OR (prev_final_update_id == L); every subsequent update must satisfy prev_final_update_id == previous final_update_id; violation -> sequence gap, fail closed
cryptohftdata.spot_continuity: after snapshot(last_update_id=L): skip updates with final_update_id <= L; first applied update must satisfy first_update_id <= L+1 <= final_update_id; every subsequent update must satisfy first_update_id == previous final_update_id + 1; if prev_final_update_id is non-null it must equal previous final_update_id; violation -> sequence gap, fail closed
cryptohftdata.duplicates_overlap_reset: byte-identical duplicate event (same ids, same level rows) -> dropped and counted; partial overlap or backwards ids -> incompatible_overlap, fail closed; a mid-stream snapshot with last_update_id >= last applied final_update_id -> state reset (counted); a snapshot with last_update_id < last applied final_update_id or a watermark-regressing snapshot -> stale/backwards snapshot, fail closed (no future or past snapshot may seed)
cryptohftdata.missing_snapshot: a replay window whose stream contains no valid snapshot before the first update (and no carried contiguous state) -> missing_initial_snapshot, fail closed
cryptohftdata.snapshot_validity: two-sided, all prices/sizes finite and > 0, >= 5 levels per side, strictly sorted, uncrossed (recon.reseed.classify_snapshot bars); truncated/one-sided/crossed/malformed snapshot -> fail closed
cryptohftdata.hour_partitioning: hours processed in order, one file at a time (bounded memory); state carries across an hour seam only when update-ID continuity holds across the seam; a missing hourly partition inside the window, or two conflicting objects for one hour, -> fail closed
cryptohftdata.identity: every row must have the expected symbol; >= 99.9% of rows must fall inside the nominal hour on received_time or on event_time (whichever axis matches; recorded); wrong exchange/symbol/date/hour -> fail closed
cryptohftdata.output_contract: internal top-K frame: mid, microprice, bid_i_price, bid_i_size, ask_i_price, ask_i_size for i in 0..k-1, sample_ts last (float64 + int64 sample_ts) - identical to recon.orderbook.OrderBook.snapshot assembly

tick_rules.lake_measurement: for each instrument and price-bearing feed: measured d = smallest d in {0,1,2,3,4} with max(|p*10^d - round(p*10^d)|) <= 1e-6 over all prices; no such d -> malformed/off-tick prices -> anomaly cap; off-tick count at the EXPECTED scale recorded; conformance scale for native replay = 10^max(d_measured over delta+book+trades, d_expected)
tick_rules.cryptohftdata_measurement: prices parsed as decimal.Decimal from the vendor string; tick exactness is exact integer arithmetic at the measured decimal count; comparison space = integer ticks at the shared conformance scale
tick_rules.precision: vendor decimal strings are never round-tripped through float before tick conversion; internal frame floats are produced as ticks/scale once, deterministically

integrity_definitions.crossed_sample: best_bid >= best_ask at a grid sample (established book only, post-seed)
integrity_definitions.missing_sample: no top-of-book on at least one side at a grid sample
integrity_definitions.thin_sample: present and uncrossed but fewer than k=10 levels on at least one side
integrity_definitions.frozen_run: maximal run of >= 60 consecutive 1-second grid samples whose full top-K state (all 4k price/size values, presence pattern) is identical; frozen_fraction = samples inside such runs / n_samples
integrity_definitions.silence: inter-event gap on the resolved engine-time axis of the delta stream; recorded at > 10s, > 60s, > 300s
integrity_definitions.stale_but_uncrossed: a frozen_run whose samples are present and uncrossed (subset of frozen accounting)

lake_day_quality.source: frozen production bars: scripts/run_binance_recon.py::Thresholds == run_coinbase_quality_map thresholds (docs 5a-QualityMap); NOT chosen by this experiment

anomaly_caps.off_tick: no d <= 4 renders all prices integral -> verdict capped at degraded
anomaly_caps.silence_cap_effect: any book_delta_v2 inter-event gap > 300s -> verdict capped at degraded

hard_invalidators.input_identity: raw unit sha256 or schema fingerprint != fixture pin -> inconclusive (wrong input)
hard_invalidators.schema_drift: observed columns incompatible with expected_schemas -> inconclusive
hard_invalidators.determinism: any cross-run inequality (excluding secs/ts keys) -> inconclusive
hard_invalidators.native_conformance: python/native frame or meta divergence when the conformance arm runs -> inconclusive

chd_window_quality.note: same frozen bars applied to the CryptoHFTData reconstruction on its approved windows (seed_crossed_frac over its snapshot events)

comparison.basis: both sources sampled as-of the same exchange-time 1s grid, k=10, tick space at the shared conformance scale; agreement metrics computed over joint-valid samples; exact-tick agreement and depth overlap recorded descriptively

determinism.stage2_cli: two full runs of scripts/run_binance_recon.py with identical arguments into two separate output roots; per-unit manifest records must be equal excluding keys {secs, ts}; published parquet sha256 values must be equal
determinism.harness: in-process replays hashed with experiments.snapshot_seed.frame_replay_hash; two replays of the same inputs must produce identical frame hashes; every report carries report_hash = eval.hashing.hash_obj(report, exclude_keys=('report_hash',))

conformance.python_native: if recon_native is importable AND the measured tick scale is exact (0 off-tick at the conformance scale): run recon.native.reconstruct_lake_l2_at_samples_seeded_native with the explicit measured price_scale on the same cleaned inputs and require frame equality (assert_frame_equal, check_dtype=True) and meta equality (excluding non-semantic keys); divergence -> inconclusive; unavailable/unverified tick -> recorded skip (Python oracle remains the authority, no cap)
conformance.scope: experiment-scoped conformance evidence only; it does NOT register a Binance tick scale in recon/native.py (that is a separate reviewed change)

lake_verdict.certified: every unit meets lake_unit_requirements AND no anomaly cap fired AND no hard invalidator fired AND determinism passed AND schema/tick conformance passed
lake_verdict.degraded: units complete but any topk_l2 classifies degraded, or any anomaly cap fired (off-tick / silence / frozen), with no hard invalidator
lake_verdict.inconclusive: any hard invalidator fired, any required unit missing or errored, any topk_l2 classifies inconclusive (seed gate), or the run cannot complete

chd_verdict.certified: approved window(s) pass validation, the fail-closed replay completes with zero continuity violations, and chd_window_quality bars pass
chd_verdict.degraded: replay completes but a chd_window_quality bar fails
chd_verdict.inconclusive: validation or fail-closed replay refuses the data (missing/malformed snapshot, gap, overlap, identity failure), or the window was never approved/downloaded

final_source_decision.lake_go: lake_verdict == certified AND (comparison not executable OR comparison bars pass). If comparison bars pass: 'Crypto Lake approved for the pilot, independently validated; CryptoHFTData recorded as agreeing fallback candidate'. If CHD data was unusable (chd inconclusive on its own data): 'Crypto Lake approved on internal certification only; independent validation impossible; fallback none' - recorded explicitly
final_source_decision.chd_go: lake_verdict != certified AND chd_verdict == certified on the approved April windows: 'CryptoHFTData approved instead; #35 source and manifest contract must switch' (subject to the non-April escalation when April cannot carry the decision alone)
final_source_decision.neither: lake_verdict != certified AND chd_verdict != certified -> neither source approved; #35 stays blocked; open a separately scoped vendor/pivot decision
final_source_decision.disagreement: lake_verdict == certified AND chd_verdict == certified AND comparison bars FAIL -> the April day cannot attribute fault; NO GO is issued for either source from April data alone; escalate per escalation.dev_days

escalation.one_day_insufficient: triggered when final_source_decision routes to 'disagreement', or when both sources pass every April bar identically but an acceptance criterion remains untestable on April data
escalation.dev_day_rules: fixed a priori (mid-month, inside the six-month window, inside documented CryptoHFTData coverage, non-April); each dev day requires its own bounded, user-approved request plan for BOTH vendors before any download; on non-April dev days outcome-bearing parity (issue-54 parity family incl. label agreement) is permitted

probe.retry_policy: retry only transient HTTP (429/5xx/timeout) with exponential backoff (base 2s, cap 30s); 4xx other than 429 aborts
probe.byte_cap_effect: streamed download aborts beyond 512 MiB (fail closed; size not documented)

expansion.condition: every probe check passed AND the approval explicitly permits expansion; same per-object bounds

request_bounds.no_other_vendor_io: no additional Crypto Lake request of any kind; no CryptoHFTData request beyond the approved objects; nothing before the explicit user approval checkpoint

evidence.tracked_contents: small aggregate JSON evidence only (verdict summaries, index with sha256 pins of ignored reports and commit SHAs); never raw market rows
evidence.prereg_commit_rule: this file is committed before the decision-bearing local replay; the commit SHA is recorded in every subsequent report

amendments.note: Adversarial-review amendment, added BEFORE any CryptoHFTData object was downloaded, replayed, or compared (no CHD data exists locally) and independent of any Lake threshold: (1) STRICTLY TIGHTENING the replay_contract.cryptohftdata.monotone_watermark rule — an event that is APPLIED to the book (an accepted update, or a snapshot seed/reset) must never carry a raw event_time behind the monotone watermark; an applied regression is a HARD per-event refusal (ordering_anomaly / backwards_snapshot). The 0.1% aggregate regression bound now applies only to non-applied events (pre-snapshot skips, deduplicated duplicates). Rationale: the aggregate bound alone would let a single time-reordered stale update whose ids straddle a reset snapshot's book version mutate a freshly reseeded book silently. (2) Enforcement hardening of decision_logic.lake_verdict (no bar changed): the verdict aggregator only consults Stage-2 manifest records whose dt equals fixture.lake.day, requires each classification-bearing topk_l2 record to have been produced under the preregistered reconstruction contract (k/grid_ms/book_stride_ms/policy/thresholds/schema_version), and requires a replay-conformance report for BOTH instruments — any absence or mismatch is inconclusive.

Clarifying amendment, added BEFORE any CryptoHFTData data was seen: the CHD adapter hard-refuses EVERY non-ok snapshot (crossed/one_sided/thin_depth/bad_values -> fail-closed inconclusive), which is STRICTER than the Lake seed gate (which counts crossed candidates into seed_source_crossed_frac). Consequently, on any CHD replay that completes, seed_source_crossed_frac is structurally 0.0 and the chd_window_quality.seed_crossed_frac_max bar is vacuous-by-strictness; it is retained for structural parity with the Lake bars and the reported value must be read as 'no non-ok snapshot was tolerated', not as a measured tolerance. Thresholds unchanged.

Execution-engine feasibility amendment, added BEFORE any Stage-2 replay metric was observed (the first Python-oracle perp replay was still in flight and had emitted no output; the only real-data results seen were the already-reported verify-inputs/tick-scale/silence PASSes). The Python-oracle full-day replay of the 109,317,254-row perp book_delta_v2 day is computationally infeasible to repeat (>7 h CPU and unfinished at amendment time; the original protocol implies four such replays). NO threshold, metric definition, schema pin, or decision logic changes. Engine protocol changes only: (1) Stage-2 CLI run 1 (--engine auto -> Python oracle) remains the full-day ground-truth anchor for BOTH instruments. (2) The second production run executes the SAME production unit processing (run_binance_recon RunConfig/plan_units/unit_is_done/process_unit) with an explicit native-engine override at the measured tick scales (perp 10, spot 100; 0 off-tick across ~174M prices) via the experiment CLI stage2-native-run — recon/native.py's registry is NOT modified (that registration remains a separate reviewed change). (3) The determinism + conformance evidence is UNIFIED and strengthened: run 1 (python) and run 2 (native) manifests must be equal on every semantic field (rows, sha256 of published parquet, classification, reasons, quality metrics, seed blocks, engine_time_col, dropped_rows) excluding only {secs, ts, engine, price_scale} — full-day cross-engine byte-level equality on the production path, which is strictly stronger conformance coverage than the original in-process arm; any difference is a hard invalidator (inconclusive). (4) The in-process harness determinism arm runs the native engine twice per instrument (identical frame_replay_hash and meta required); frozen-book metrics come from that frame, which the cross-engine equality pins to the oracle's. (5) The synthetic python-vs-native conformance suite (tests/test_binance_recon_conformance.py) remains in force unchanged.
